title: Molecular Attoscope: Pulse Shape Spectroscopy of Electronic Coherence
authors: Loc Thi-Hoang Ngo, Julia Codere, Javin Ohara, Brian Kaufman, Martin G Cohen, Tamas Rozgonyi, Philipp Marquetand, Matthew Bain, Brett J Pearson, Ruaridh Forbes, Thomas Weinacht
abstract: Tracking the coupled motion of electrons and nuclei on their intrinsic timescales is essential to understanding and controlling photochemical transformations. While attosecond techniques have provided unprecedented insight into electronic dynamics, they have largely been restricted to ionic systems, with nuclear motion often neglected or indirectly inferred. Here, we demonstrate a ``molecular attoscope", which uses shaped laser pulses in the deep ultraviolet to perform a coherent measurement of electronic and nuclear dynamics in an entangled wave packet in neutral benzene. This enables us to trace both the 856-attosecond-period electronic motion and the 36-femtosecond-period nuclear motion. We observe electronic coherence persisting over hundreds of optical cycles, modulated by nuclear dynamics. Our holographic approach is general, and lays the groundwork for coherent measurements capable of visualizing the evolution of the coupled electronic-nuclear wave function in real time.
summary: The researchers developed a 'molecular attoscope' using shaped deep-ultraviolet laser pulses to observe the simultaneous motion of electrons and nuclei in benzene. This technique allows for the tracking of electronic coherence on an attosecond scale alongside nuclear motion on a femtosecond scale. This advancement provides a way to visualize the complex, entangled evolution of wave functions in neutral molecules.
main_problem: The difficulty of simultaneously tracking coupled electronic and nuclear dynamics on their respective attosecond and femtosecond timescales in neutral molecular systems.
main_result: Demonstration of a 'molecular attoscope' that traces 856-attosecond electronic motion and 36-femtosecond nuclear motion in neutral benzene, showing electronic coherence modulated by nuclear dynamics.
method: A holographic approach using shaped laser pulses in the deep ultraviolet to perform coherent measurements of an entangled wave packet.
model_or_system: An entangled electronic-nuclear wave packet in neutral benzene molecules.
important_parameters: 856-attosecond electronic period, 36-femtosecond nuclear period, deep ultraviolet pulse shaping.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the challenge of coupled dynamics, describes the implementation of the molecular attoscope using shaped DUV pulses, presents the measurement of benzene dynamics, and discusses the general applicability of the holographic approach.
why_interesting: It demonstrates a method for real-time visualization of the evolution of coupled electronic-nuclear wave functions, which is highly relevant to understanding non-adiabatic dynamics and open quantum system evolution in molecular physics.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
